assertion_results.text: Step 0 detects the absence of the Deployment Context column in the Source Repositories table and defaults all repositories to upstream (§1.78)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md lines 21-29 explicitly state: 'The Source Repositories table in CLAUDE.md does **not** include a Deployment Context column' and 'Per backward compatibility rules, all repositories default to `upstream` deployment context.' Lines 101-103 reiterate: 'The Deployment Context column is **absent** from the table. Per backward compatibility rules, the deployment context defaults to `upstream`.' Step 0 clearly detects the absence and applies the upstream default.

assertion_results.text: Remediation task descriptions do NOT include a Coordination Guidance subsection — the section is omitted entirely when no Deployment Context column exists
assertion_results.evidence: In remediation.md, Task 1's description (lines 74-116) contains sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements, Dependencies — no Coordination Guidance subsection. Task 2's description (lines 139-176) contains the same structure — no Coordination Guidance subsection. The top-level '## Coordination Guidance' section at line 203 is outside the task descriptions and explicitly confirms: 'the Coordination Guidance subsection is **omitted entirely** from the remediation task descriptions above.' data-extraction.md line 29 also confirms: 'Since the Deployment Context column is absent entirely, coordination guidance will be **omitted** from remediation task descriptions.'

assertion_results.text: The triage proceeds normally through all steps without errors from the missing Deployment Context column
assertion_results.evidence: The triage completes all steps without any errors: Step 0 (data-extraction.md lines 1-30, configuration validation and deployment context handling), Step 0.3 (matrix staleness check, line 38-40), Step 0.7 (assignment, lines 42-61), Step 1 (data extraction, lines 65-103), Step 2 (version impact analysis, remediation.md lines 1-26), Step 3 (affects versions correction, lines 28-43), Step 8 (remediation with task creation, lines 45-222). No error messages or failures appear anywhere in either output file. The missing Deployment Context column is handled gracefully at each point where it is referenced.

assertion_results.text: Backward compatibility is maintained — existing eval behavior is unaffected by the absence of the Deployment Context column
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md lines 203-205 explicitly state: 'This maintains backward compatibility -- existing workflows that predate the Deployment Context feature are unaffected.' data-extraction.md line 29 references 'backward compatibility rules' for the default. All standard triage behaviors are present and unaffected: version impact analysis produces a full table, affects versions correction is proposed, two remediation tasks (upstream backport + downstream propagation) are created with complete descriptions, Jira linkage is proposed, and post-triage summary is planned. The only change is the omission of the Coordination Guidance subsection, which is the intended backward-compatible behavior.
